noops.rationale: Marker sizes are converted with the point scale owned by the axes; an unrelated renderer DPI cannot change that stored geometry.
The shim has one deterministic text metric approximation and no renderer-specific font-metric object to consult.
Axes limit callbacks are not part of the supported shim API, so changing callback emission cannot affect the resulting view.
The shim does not implement nested subfigures; the immediate and root figure are therefore the same supported object.
There is one stored figure-fraction axes rectangle and no active-versus-original transform graph in the shim.
The engine always derives auto limits directly from entries plus the requested margin, with no separate sticky-edge tightness pass.
Visibility changes presentation opacity but deliberately retain every entry in the data-extent model used by relim.
The supported aspect model has no independent layout-anchor graph, and shared axes already resolve through shared axis state.
External facet legends are observable only with hue mapping, which this bounded FacetGrid surface rejects explicitly.
The shim exposes no figure observer registry, so both values perform the same complete supported-state reset.
Contour labels use deterministic shim-owned placement and styling; unsupported manual layout controls cannot alter that bounded output.
FixedFormatter selects its predeclared label exclusively by tick position, matching its supported sequence contract.
FixedLocator returns its configured locations regardless of the current view bounds; clipping is performed by the axis renderer.
Percent-style formatting depends only on the numeric value and has no position placeholder in its supported contract.
The bounded log locator emits every in-view requested-decade tick and leaves density clipping to the axis layout.
Edge ticks remain part of the locator result and are clipped at draw time, so constructor pruning does not alter locations.
NullFormatter intentionally returns an empty label for every numeric value and every tick position.
NullLocator intentionally returns no locations for every possible pair of view bounds.
Scalar formatting is a pure numeric conversion and does not vary according to the tick's sequence position.
Date label text is derived from epoch milliseconds and the stored format, independent of tick sequence position.
The supported date contract is naive UTC plain text, without timezone conversion or a TeX rendering backend.
Calendar locators operate in the shim's documented naive UTC millisecond coordinate space.
Calendar locators operate in the shim's documented naive UTC millisecond coordinate space.
Calendar locators operate in the shim's documented naive UTC millisecond coordinate space.
